changeReason: Improved translation from Japanese. Old: "The Investigation Runs Aground"

TextENGNew: "...Yes. Yesterday, when we met at the restaurant in Ginza,@k@vS20/00/kataoka44002.the subject came up."
"That's them overstepping their authority. They're meddling far too much in Public Safety's affairs.@k@vS20/00/442600002.If we keep kowtowing, we'll set a dangerous precedent."
changeReason: The new translation changes the subject of 'kowtowing' from 'they' to 'we' (matching JPN 迎合する) and introduces the idea of subservience/pandering rather than generic bad precedent-setting.

TextENGNew: "It seems Minister Inukai is openly displeased with Public Safety's activities. The Vice-Minister will probably raise the issue at Monday's agency meeting.@k@vS20/00/kataoka44004....What a headache..."
changeReason: The old translates 庁議 as 'debate session' when it means 'agency meeting,' and renders まいったなぁ as resigned acceptance rather than the frustration ('What a headache') conveyed in the Japanese.

TextENGNew: ``We can only have the Director address the Vice-Minister. That's why we pay him the big bucks.``
``Well, if it comes to that, they're going to ask him for proof that the Minister's being threatened.``
"Sometimes I can't tell whose side that man is on. If he's not backing us, what's the point?"

"Well, the pressure is probably coming down through the Minister. All we can do is push the investigation forward while he sweats it out in the Vice-Minister's office, buying us time.@k@vS20/00/kataoka44007....He might get wrung so dry there's nothing left of him, though."
changeReason: The old translation says pressure is directed at the Chief from the Minister, while the JPN 大臣を経由しての圧力 and new translation correctly convey pressure channeled through the Minister from above.

TextENGNew: ...So, how's the investigation going?``
"Every promising lead I was counting on turned out to be a dead end. Honestly, I'm at a loss."
``There are reports that the Hinamizawa Dam Project has been showing up a lot lately in the Minister's press releases, so what about that?``

"...Hmm... 'Showing up' is a bit strong -- it's more of an extremely faint sense that something's off.@k@vS20/00/442600007.It happened to come up during his speech at the Prefectural Forum, so it may have just been a topical reference."
``If I remember correctly, a local organization there was protesting quite violently, no? The Onigafuchi Guardians.@k@vS20/00/kataoka44011. I think Akasaka-kun was investigating that.``
"I believe those people couldn't have pulled off this incident. But since nearly every other suspicious lead has come up clean, I may not be able to rule them out either..."
changeReason: Old reverses the meaning: says they 'could possibly be behind' the incident, but Japanese says they could NOT have pulled it off.

TextENGNew: "Akasaka-kun submitted a brief report saying the possibility couldn't be dismissed."
``...Then, isn't there merit in investigating them? We're going through everything with a fine-toothed comb, so if they're one of the suspicious groups that're left, we'll eventually have to.``

"Well, they're hardly the only group we should be looking at, are they? Ahahaha!@k@vS20/00/442600011.If we're talking about organizations that are equally as suspicious as the Onigafuchi group, the list is considerable.@k@vS20/00/442600012.We don't have the manpower, the time, or the overtime budget for it all."
changeReason: The new translation specifies 'overtime budget' (matching JPN 残業代, overtime pay) instead of the generic 'money' in the old translation.

TextENGNew: "Try getting more details from Akasaka-kun. ...I have a feeling we should keep a close eye on those Guardians."
changeReason: The old translation has the Chief saying he will ask Akasaka-kun himself, while the JPN 聞いてみてよ is a command directed at Kanou to do it, which the new translation correctly conveys.

TextENGNew: "He reported that he's successfully made contact with the villagers. Apparently he's also coordinating with the local police."
"Keep close contact with Akasaka-kun. And if anything in his reports raises a flag, we may send reinforcements."
changeReason: The old translation says to keep contact 'on the down-low' (secret), while the JPN 連絡、密にしてください means to keep contact frequent/close, which the new translation correctly conveys.

TextENGNew: ``Got it.
...Ah, sorry... Chief Kataoka~! There's a phone call from the Director.@k@vS20/00/442600016. Should I transfer it over here?``
``Ah, no, it's okay! I'll head over there.@k@vS20/00/kataoka44016. ...Yes hello?@k@vS20/00/kataoka44017. Kataoka here.``
